notes: SYM-001: Symbotic FY2025 10-K, as of 2025-09-27. This is a BACKLOG figure, not recognized revenue -- see spec section 8.5/14.

Customer-by-customer split is NOT separately disclosed; the 10-K states Walmart and GreenBox together comprise 'the vast majority' of backlog. This scenario assumes an equal 40/40/20 split as one of several sensitivity cases, per spec section 14's instruction to provide sensitivity cases rather than one estimate presented as fact.
GreenBox is a 35% (Symbotic) / 65% (SoftBank) joint venture (SYM-002), not a fully independent commercial counterparty -- relevant when interpreting 'largest customer.'
Residual = disclosed total (SYM-001) minus this scenario's assumed Walmart and GreenBox allocations.
Alternative sensitivity case weighting Walmart's Master Automation Agreement (SYM-004: up to 400 committed + 200 optional micro-fulfillment systems, Jan 2025) more heavily.
Same GreenBox JV structure note as above (SYM-002).
Residual = disclosed total minus this scenario's assumed allocations.

Uses GreenBox's disclosed $7.5B/6-year minimum purchase commitment (SYM-003) as a floor-bound proxy for its backlog share. This is an assumption about how a forward purchase commitment maps to the current backlog snapshot, not a disclosed backlog split -- the $7.5B figure itself is disclosed, its use here as a share of the September 2025 backlog is not.
Assumed to fill most of the remainder alongside GreenBox, consistent with the 10-K's 'vast majority' language.
Residual = disclosed total minus this scenario's GreenBox floor and assumed Walmart allocation.
